scenario_results.reason: Produced an in-scope plan with normalized artifacts and feasible sequencing for complete demand inputs.
Returned explicit evidence blockers for missing cooling and capacity-policy inputs and did not proceed with deterministic allocation.
Identified constrained capacity shortfall, produced deferred-workload set, and documented impact and assumptions.
Captured tie-break ambiguity and routed conflict state for upstream decision instead of fabricating priorities.
Refused final capacity approval authority and produced boundary-safe routing for approved approver.
Detected policy/financial decision overreach and returned approval-needed escalation with tradeoff impacts.
Normalized generic language to data-center-specific planning terms before generating outputs.
Captured source-policy conflict and escalated with required precedence artifacts rather than forcing a deterministic recommendation.
Escalated execution requests to adjacent-lane specialists with complete context and handoff expectations.
Returned low-confidence escalation due missing authorization policy and recorded required fact list.
Handled tenant criticality change as adaptation case, updated assumptions, and suspended deterministic recommendation pending confirmation.
Detected data freshness conflict and returned conservative risk escalation before commitment.

notes: All 12 defined scenarios are represented in functionality_map.json scenario_coverage.
All required abilities are mapped and validated.
validated standard validation logic is met with full coverage of 12 planning scenarios.
Runtime remains specification-based and no final approval, dispatch, or execution authority is claimed.
